rationale: Abstract Y is superior because its explicit OBJECTIVE section sharpens the falsifiable aim, its structured BACKGROUND/OBJECTIVE/METHODS/RESULTS/CONCLUSION headers match PACE house style precisely, and its conclusion adds a clinically actionable recommendation calibrated appropriately to the retrospective design.
loser_weakness: Abstract X lacked a distinct OBJECTIVE section and a structured-abstract format, reducing clarity and journal fit, and its conclusion stopped at 'support S-ICD use' without the actionable clinical recommendation that the data supported.
